relatedness_reasoning: The inbound file provides eligible customer data and product information from Investments to Marketing; the outbound file is a campaign details document from Marketing to Customer Services. While the outbound file represents a downstream transformation of the eligible customer cohort into a marketing campaign, only a subset of outbound fields have clear sources in the inbound schema, and many outbound fields (budget, channels, success metrics, training deadlines) are campaign-planning artifacts not present in the eligibility data.

mappings.reasoning: No campaign identifier exists in the inbound eligibility file; this is system-generated by the marketing system.
Campaign name likely derives from the product name being promoted to eligible customers.
The inbound target_department (Marketing) becomes the outbound department_from, as Marketing creates the campaign.
Customer Services is the receiving department for campaign execution; not present in the inbound eligibility data.
Campaign launch date is a planning decision not derivable from the eligibility generation timestamp.
Campaign conclusion date is a planning decision not present in the inbound data.
Campaign type (product category) can be inferred from the investment product name in the inbound metadata.
Age range demographic data is not present in the inbound eligible_customers schema.
Account type classification is not provided in the inbound customer records.
Income level is not present in the inbound data; only account_balance is available.
Geographical region codes are not present in the inbound eligible_customers schema.
Campaign business objectives are marketing planning decisions not derivable from eligibility criteria.
Budget currency (GBP) is a campaign financial planning parameter not present in the inbound data.
Campaign budget allocation is a marketing planning decision not derivable from customer eligibility data.
Channel-specific budget allocation is a marketing planning decision not present in the inbound schema.
Social media budget allocation is a marketing planning decision not present in the inbound schema.
Digital ads budget allocation is a marketing planning decision not present in the inbound schema.
In-app notification budget allocation is a marketing planning decision not present in the inbound schema.
Marketing message content is a creative planning decision not derivable from customer eligibility data.
Expected inquiry volume may be estimated from the total count of eligible customers targeted by the campaign.
Supported languages are a customer service operational decision not present in the inbound eligibility data.
Priority support topics may be inferred from the investment product being promoted, but this is speculative.
Escalation thresholds are customer service operational policies not present in the inbound data.
Training deadline is a campaign execution planning decision not derivable from eligibility data.
Conversion targets may be set based on the size of the eligible customer cohort, but this is a planning decision.
Target engagement rate is a campaign success metric set during planning, not derivable from eligibility data.
Target satisfaction score is a campaign success metric set during planning, not present in the inbound data.
Campaign creation date may align with the eligibility list generation date, but they are distinct events.
The user who created the campaign details is not present in the inbound eligibility data.
Document version identifier is a system-generated metadata field not present in the inbound schema.
